title: terminalia cacoucia
intro: Terminalia cacoucia, also known as the Black Pui tree, is a plant species native to the tropical forests of the Western Ghats in India. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Terminalia cacoucia include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and act as an antioxidant. Herbal preparations of the plant include its use in decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
